desc: Tamarin is a west-coast Mauritius base known for surf, dolphin cruises, and easier access to Black River Gorges and Le Morne than the busier north.
hls: Gateway to Black River Gorges hikes
Strong villa and aparthotel stock for longer stays

todo.name: Tamarin Bay sunrise paddle
todo.desc: Join an early-morning paddle or kayak before winds build.

todo.name: Le Morne day trip
todo.desc: Pair beach time with mountain viewpoints on the southwest peninsula.

todo.name: Black River Gorges hike
todo.desc: Choose a half-day trail with endemic forest lookouts.

todo.name: Casela nature park activity block
todo.desc: Add zipline or safari-style activities nearby.

todo.name: Flic-en-Flac evening food stop
todo.desc: Sample street snacks and Creole seafood plates.

todo.name: Dolphin-watch boat outing
todo.desc: Book licensed operators with responsible wildlife distance policies.

wifi.note: Good in most villas/hotels; verify speeds for remote work stays.

prac.fltNote: Mauritius has regular nonstop London service and broad one-stop UK/Ireland options; peak demand is strongest in European school holidays and December festive travel.
prac.currency: Mauritius local currency (check live rates before booking).
prac.lang: Local language plus tourism-level English in main visitor areas.
prac.tz: Check local timezone and DST rules for exact travel dates.
prac.visa: Confirm current entry/transit rules for your passport before booking.
alerts: Lagoon excursions are weather and swell dependent; avoid fixed schedules for marine activities.
Road traffic toward Port Louis can be heavy on weekday mornings and evenings.
Sun and UV are intense year-round—plan midday shade even in cooler months.

sweet: May-Nov for drier, less humid conditions and reliable west-coast sunsets.
